principles: brief-thin human surfaces with machine-readable JSON truth
modular repos under a shared governance layer

projects.name: ATLAS

desiredState.ownerIntent: Foundation should project ATLAS coordination truth without replacing the stack root or treating it like an application runtime.

projects.name: Fawxzzy Fitness

desiredState.summary: Fitness should remain the primary application proving ground for deployment proof, privacy posture, and Supabase governance.
desiredState.ownerIntent: Track clean production proof and conservative private-data posture without moving Fitness implementation truth into Foundation.

observedState.summary: Foundation observes a verified public repo, READY production deployment, current deployment proof, and an active Supabase project with advisor evidence.

warnings: Wave 1A search_path hardening and Wave 1C SECURITY DEFINER execute-permission remediation are now proved in Supabase advisor evidence, but leaked password protection still blocks stronger privacy posture claims.
Separate Fitness performance remediation lanes remain tracked for unindexed foreign keys, auth_rls_initplan policy overhead, and unused index review.
Historical prod-deploy mapping remains documented until active inventory or remediation changes.
healthState.summary: Deployment proof is clean and the two SQL remediation waves are proved, but the remaining auth operator finding and tracked performance lanes still keep Fitness privacy and database posture below healthy.

dimensions.label: Evidence completeness
dimensions.summary: Split-state truth, production deployment evidence, and Supabase observation data are all present.

dimensions.label: Proof freshness
dimensions.summary: Deployment proof is current and clean on the primary Fitness Vercel project.

dimensions.label: Deployment posture
dimensions.summary: Primary production deployment is READY, while the legacy prod-deploy lane is retained only as historical inventory.

dimensions.label: Data and security posture
dimensions.summary: Wave 1A cleared the four search_path findings and Wave 1C cleared the SECURITY DEFINER execute-permission warnings, but leaked password protection and the tracked performance remediation lanes still block stronger privacy and data-security claims.

warnings: Wave 1A search_path hardening and Wave 1C SECURITY DEFINER execute-permission remediation are now proved in Supabase advisor evidence, but leaked password protection still blocks stronger privacy posture claims.
Separate Fitness performance remediation lanes remain tracked for unindexed foreign keys, auth_rls_initplan policy overhead, and unused index review.
Historical prod-deploy mapping remains documented until active inventory or remediation changes.
scorecard.nextAction: Enable Fitness leaked password protection through the Supabase auth operator lane, keep the separate performance remediation lanes moving, and require reviewed advisor proof before changing posture.
